label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which of the four options is true based on the given passage about computers and the Kurzweil Reading Machine. First, let me read the passage again to make sure I understand all the details. The passage starts by talking about the various uses of computers, from solving difficult problems to controlling machines. It mentions calculators for simple math, large computers for handling many problems at once. Then it lists other uses like gaming and learning languages. The main focus then shifts to the Kurzweil Reading Machine, which helps blind people by reading books for them. The process involves placing the book on a scanner, and the machine reads aloud, either normally or in a fast voice. It can handle different text sizes. The machine is useful for various reading materials, including newspapers, magazines, books, and letters. Libraries have these machines, and they also help short-sighted children learn to read. Now the question is to pick the correct statement from four options: A) Computers can solve all the problems people can't do. B) Large computers can only solve one problem at a time. C) When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything. D) The Kurzweil machine can help blind people and short-sighted children. Let me check each option against the passage. Starting with option A: "Computers can solve all the problems people can't do." The passage mentions computers can solve difficult problems, but it doesn't say they can solve all problems people can't. The wording here is too absolute. The passage says "many uses" and "solve difficult problems" but doesn't claim "all problems people can't do". So A is likely false. Option B: "Large computers can only solve one problem at a time." The passage states that scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time. The wording here in B is the opposite. So B is false. Option C: "When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything." The passage says that a blind person has to open the book, put it on the scanner, turn the page, and put it back.
